name: panel-review
description: Multi-model second-opinion deliberation for high-stakes coding and financial decisions. Eight skills across two profiles — coding and financial (audit / deliberate / synthesize per profile), plus shared record-outcome and skip-gate — route to the TruVerifAI MCP server, where four frontier models reason independently and conflict-target each other's positions to produce decision-grade output. Outcome reporting closes the loop — the dashboard shows what % of MCP calls actually changed the agent's decision.

api_token.title: TruVerifAI API key
api_token.description: Your tvai_... key. Sign up at https://truverif.ai/signup and generate a key at https://truverif.ai/settings/api-keys.

enable_gates.title: Enable the proactive review gates
enable_gates.description: When enabled, a PreToolUse gate runs before a risky git commit (audit) or a risky design write (deliberate). A local classifier scores the change across many domains (auth, billing, migrations, secrets, infra-as-code, and more — including risky DELETIONS like a removed permission check) and sends TruVerifAI only a repo fingerprint + hunk content hashes — never source, paths, or diffs. If the change isn't already covered the gate asks the agent to call audit_coding / deliberate_coding first, OR to record a one-line skip-with-reason. Fails open on any error and never deadlocks. Set to false to disable the gates entirely. Default true. To change it, type true or false.

deliberate_mode.title: Deliberate gate strictness
deliberate_mode.description: 'tiered' (default) blocks only high-confidence/irreversible design forks (schema, migrations, auth, dependencies, IaC exposure) and routes lower-confidence borderline changes via borderline_mode (below); 'block' blocks every risky design write; 'advisory' never blocks (surfaces a suggestion only). A blocked write releases by running deliberate_coding OR calling record_gate_skip. The audit gate (pre-commit) is unaffected. To change it, type one of: tiered, block, or advisory.

borderline_mode.title: Borderline (low-confidence) tier
borderline_mode.description: How to handle borderline changes — consequential but not high-confidence-risky (e.g. a new public API surface, a notable refactor). 'advisory' (default) surfaces a fast synthesize_coding suggestion to the agent without blocking (shown once per area per session for the highest-signal 'heavy' spikes); 'synthesize_gate' soft-gates the highest-signal borderline changes (releasable by running synthesize_coding OR calling record_gate_skip with a reason_code); 'off' ignores borderline entirely. Never hard-blocks like the audit gate. To change it, type one of: advisory, synthesize_gate, or off.

gate_threshold.title: Gate trigger threshold (advanced)
gate_threshold.description: Raise this to make the gates fire less often in a noisy repo. Higher = fewer borderline triggers. It is FLOOR-BOUNDED: the value is clamped to a safe ceiling, so genuinely high-stakes changes (auth, secrets, migrations, removed permission/validation checks) ALWAYS fire and hardcoded secrets always fire — you cannot silently disable the gate this way (use enable_gates=false for that, which is visible). Leave blank for the calibrated default. To change it, type a whole number (e.g. 28).

borderline_sampling_rate.title: Borderline soft-gate sampling rate (advanced)
borderline_sampling_rate.description: Only used when borderline_mode='synthesize_gate'. The fraction (0.0–1.0) of borderline-heavy changes that actually soft-gate; the rest get an advisory nudge. Keeps the soft-gate from over-firing on a high-volume band. Default 0.5. To change it, type a decimal between 0 and 1.

borderline_session_budget.title: Borderline soft-gate session budget (advanced)
borderline_session_budget.description: Only used when borderline_mode='synthesize_gate'. The maximum number of borderline soft-gates per session; after the cap, all borderline degrades to advisory. Prevents alarm fatigue. Default 3. Type 0 to disable the cap. To change it, type a whole number.
